run.command: bun run scripts/measure-locomo-union-live.ts --benchmark-root <root> --union-topk 16 --with-extraction --concurrency 6 --run-id run-p4-full10-union16-ext-live --resume (root: bun run scripts/prepare-phase-65-locomo-data.ts -- --output-root <root> --max-conversations 10 --max-questions-per-case 0; baseline: same script with --no-memory, run-id run-p4-full10-no-memory-baseline)

metrics.primary: answer accuracy, deterministic token-F1 scoring (judge-free), full 10-conversation LoCoMo set (1986 questions)

coverage.note: full public LoCoMo-10: 10 conversations, 1986 questions (single_hop 841, multi_hop 282, temporal 321, open_domain 96, adversarial 446); per-category accuracy 0.683/0.436/0.679/0.281/0.648, evidence recall overall 0.769

claimBoundary.reason: Judge-free deterministic token-F1 scoring, executionFailures 0 over all 1986 questions, resumable per-question checkpointing. Profile disclosed: opt-in retrieval.semanticCandidates union (topK 16, provider embedding text-embedding-3-small) + conversational write-time extraction + abstention-format answer prompt (answerSystem union-live-abstention-format-v1). No-memory ablation on the identical scorer/root: overall 0.2276 and non-adversarial 0.0045 (7/1540) vs with-memory non-adversarial 0.6117 (942/1540) - the non-adversarial set is unanswerable without memory. Disclosure: the adversarial category (446 questions, gold = abstain) is trivially aced by the no-memory arm (0.998 vs with-memory 0.648), so the headline memory lift must cite the non-adversarial split, not the adversarial one. Dataset is CC BY-NC 4.0: any public claim must disclose the non-commercial scope. Embedding-free boundary stands separately: rules-only lexical retrieval scored 0.020 on conv-1 (banked P65-R003); this claim is for the embedding+extraction profile. README promoted 2026-07-04 with user sign-off: public-claims row + disclosure paragraph added to README.md and README.zh-CN.md in the same change; the internal-diagnostics conv-1 boundary row was removed as superseded by this full-run claim (the 0.020 rules-only boundary remains disclosed here and in the public paragraph).
